etymology.description: semantic 木(木) (mù, "wood") + phonetic 爾(爾) (ěr) -> wooden tool -> loom
etymology.interpretation: 檷(nǐ, "loom") combines 木(木) (mù, "wood"), indicating the material traditionally used in construction, with the phonetic element 爾(爾) (ěr) to convey the pronunciation. The loom, being primarily made of wood, aligns with the semantic contribution of 木(木), building the idea of a structure used in weaving.
